revision.text:
'''葛城国造'''（かずらきのくにのみやつこ・かずらきこくぞう・かつらぎのくにのみやつこ・かつらぎこくぞう)は[[大和国]]南西部を支配した[[国造]]。葛木国造とも。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === [[剣根|剣根命]]。[[高魂命|高皇産霊尊]]の5世孫または[[天押立命]]の4世孫という。神武天皇2年に初めて葛城国造とされた。初代[[倭国造]]とされる[[椎根津彦|椎根津彦命]]とともに国造の最も古い例とされる。
=== 氏族 === 葛城氏。[[姓]]は[[直]]。天武天皇12年([[683年]])に[[連]]に、14年に[[忌寸]]に昇格した。5世紀に繁栄した葛城氏は[[武内宿祢|武内宿禰]]の子孫で、姓も[[臣]]で別系統だが、婚姻関係によって同系統化している。
=== 本拠 === 大和国葛城地方。葛上郡・葛下郡両郡。現在の奈良県御所市・五條市など。
=== 氏神 === 不明。同地域には[[葛城御縣神社|葛城御県神社]]・[[葛城一言主神社|葛城坐一言主神社]]など延喜の制の名神大社・大社が連立。
=== 子孫 === *[[葛城荒田彦]] …… [[古墳時代]]の伝説上の人。国造。娘の葛比売が神別葛城氏の襲津彦に嫁いだ。 *[[葛城磐村]] …… [[古墳時代]]の人。娘の広子は[[用明天皇]]の嬪となった。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) *『神道大辞典』([[臨川書店]]) *[[舎人親王]]『[[日本書紀]]』
[[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目|かすらきのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:大和国|かすらきのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史|かすらきのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:古墳時代|かすらきのくにのみやつこ]]

=== 氏族 === 葛城氏。[[姓]]は[[直]]。天武天皇12年([[683年]])に[[連]]に、14年に[[忌寸]]に昇格した。5世紀に繁栄した葛城氏は[[武内宿禰]]の子孫で、姓も[[臣]]で別系統だが、婚姻関係によって同系統化している。
=== 本拠 === 大和国[[葛城]]地方。[[葛上郡]]・[[葛下郡]]両郡。現在の[[奈良県]][[御所市]]・[[大和高田市]]など。
=== 氏神 === 不明。同地域には[[葛城御縣神社|葛城御県神社]]・[[葛城一言主神社|葛城坐一言主神社]]など延喜の制の名神大社・大社が連立。
=== 子孫 === *[[葛城荒田彦]] …… [[古墳時代]]の伝説上の人。国造。娘の葛比売は神別葛城氏の[[葛城襲津彦]]の母。 *[[葛城磐村]] …… [[古墳時代]]の人。娘の広子は[[用明天皇]]の嬪となった。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == * 『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) * 『神道大辞典』([[臨川書店]]) * [[舎人親王]]『[[日本書紀]]』 {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: /* 概要 */修正
revision.text:
'''葛城国造'''（かずらきのくにのみやつこ・かずらきこくぞう・かつらぎのくにのみやつこ・かつらぎこくぞう)は[[大和国]]南西部を支配した[[国造]]。葛木国造とも言う。
== 概要 == 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」では'''葛城国造'''とある。
=== 祖先 === *『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」には'''[[高皇産霊尊]]'''の5世孫または[[天押立命]]の4世孫（実際は3世孫）の'''[[剣根命]]'''が[[神武天皇]]朝に国造に任命されたと伝わる。初代[[倭国造]]とされる[[椎根津彦|椎根津彦命]]、[[紀国造]]の[[天道根命]]、[[伊勢国造]]の[[天日別命]]、[[素賀国造]]の[[美志印命]]らとともに国造の最も古い例とされる。 *『先代旧事本紀』「天皇本紀」では神武天皇二年に'''[[八咫烏]]'''が表彰され、子孫が[[葛野県主]]に任命されたとする。
=== 氏族 === '''[[葛城氏 (葛城国造)|葛城]]'''（かつらぎうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）。[[天武天皇]]12年([[683年]])に[[連]]を、14年に[[忌寸]]を下賜された。[[5世紀]]に繁栄した[[葛城氏]]は[[武内宿禰]]の子孫で、姓も[[臣]]で別系統だが、婚姻関係によって同系統化している。
== 本拠 == 本拠。
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''葛城国'''と呼ばれた地域で、のちの大和国'''[[葛城]]'''地方（[[葛上郡]]・[[葛下郡]]両郡）にあたる。現在の[[奈良県]][[御所市]]・[[大和高田市]]。
== 氏神 == 不明。同地域には[[葛城御縣神社|葛城御県神社]]・[[葛城一言主神社|葛城坐一言主神社]]など延喜の制の名神大社・大社が連立。
== 子孫 == *[[葛城荒田彦]] *:[[古墳時代]]の人物で葛城国造。娘の[[葛比売]]は神別葛城氏の[[葛城襲津彦]]の母。 *[[葛城磐村]] *:古墳時代の人。娘の広子は[[用明天皇]]の嬪となった。 *女（名称不明） *:[[蘇我稲目]]の妻（[[蘇我馬子]]の母）は葛城氏の出身と考えられている。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造#一覧|国造の一覧]] *[[倭の六県]]
== 参考文献 == * 『[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|國史大辭典]]』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) * 『[[神道大辞典]]』([[臨川書店]]) * [[舎人親王]]『[[日本書紀]]』 {{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:かすらきのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:大和国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:葛城氏|*]]

== 子孫 == *[[葛城荒田彦]] *:[[古墳時代]]の人物で葛城国造。娘の[[葛比売]]は神別葛城氏の[[葛城襲津彦]]の母。 *[[葛城磐村]] *:古墳時代の人。娘の広子は[[用明天皇]]の嬪となった。 *女（名称不明） *:[[蘇我稲目]]の妻（[[蘇我馬子]]の母）は葛城氏の出身と考えられている。

'''葛城国造'''（かずらきのくにのみやつこ・かずらきこくぞう・かつらぎのくにのみやつこ・かつらぎこくぞう）は[[葛城国]]（[[令制国]]の[[大和国]]南西部）を支配した[[国造]]。葛木国造とも言う。
== 概要 == 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」では'''葛城国造'''とある。
=== 祖先 === *『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」には'''[[高皇産霊尊]]'''の5世孫または[[天押立命]]の4世孫（実際は3世孫）の'''[[剣根命]]'''が[[神武天皇]]朝に国造に任命されたと伝わる。初代[[倭国造]]とされる[[椎根津彦|椎根津彦命]]、[[紀国造]]の[[天道根命]]、[[伊勢国造]]の[[天日別命]]、[[素賀国造]]の[[美志印命]]らとともに国造の最も古い例とされる。 *『先代旧事本紀』「天皇本紀」では神武天皇二年に'''[[八咫烏]]'''が表彰され、子孫が[[葛野県主]]に任命されたとする。
=== 氏族 === '''[[葛城氏 (葛城国造)|葛城]]'''（かつらぎうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）。[[天武天皇]]12年([[683年]])に[[連]]を、14年に[[忌寸]]を下賜された。[[5世紀]]に繁栄した[[葛城氏]]は[[武内宿禰]]の子孫で、姓も[[臣]]で別系統だが、婚姻関係によって同系統化している。

== 氏神 == 不明。同地域には[[葛城御縣神社|葛城御県神社]]・[[葛城一言主神社|葛城坐一言主神社]]など延喜の制の名神大社・大社が連立。
== 人物 == *'''[[荒田彦]]'''（あらたひこ） *:古墳時代の人物で、娘の[[葛比売]]は皇別葛城氏の[[葛城襲津彦]]の母。
== 子孫 == *[[葛城磐村]] *:古墳時代の人。娘の広子は[[用明天皇]]の嬪となった。 *女（名称不明） *:[[蘇我稲目]]の妻（[[蘇我馬子]]の母）は葛城氏の出身と考えられている。 *[[葛城山田瑞子]] *:[[欽明天皇|欽明朝]]の人。稲目と共に[[児島郡]]へ田令として派遣された。孫の息海は児島[[評造]]となる。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造#一覧|国造の一覧]] *[[倭の六県]]
== 参考文献 == * 『[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|國史大辭典]]』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) * 『[[神道大辞典]]』([[臨川書店]]) * [[舎人親王]]『[[日本書紀]]』

'''葛城国造'''（かずらきのくにのみやつこ・かずらきこくぞう・かつらぎのくにのみやつこ・かつらぎこくぞう）は[[葛城国]]（[[令制国]]の[[大和国]]南西部）を支配した[[国造]]。葛木国造とも言う。
== 概要 == 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」では'''葛城国造'''とある。
=== 祖先 === *『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」には'''[[高皇産霊尊]]'''の5世孫または[[天押立命]]の4世孫（実際は3世孫）の'''[[剣根命]]'''が[[神武天皇]]朝に国造に任命されたと伝わる。初代[[倭国造]]とされる[[椎根津彦|椎根津彦命]]、[[紀国造]]の[[天道根命]]、[[伊勢国造]]の[[天日別命]]、[[素賀国造]]の[[美志印命]]らとともに国造の最も古い例とされる。 *『先代旧事本紀』「天皇本紀」では神武天皇二年に'''[[八咫烏]]'''が表彰され、子孫が[[葛野県主]]に任命されたとする。
=== 氏族 === '''[[葛城氏 (葛城国造)|葛城]]'''（かつらぎうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）。[[天武天皇]]12年([[683年]])に[[連]]を、14年に[[忌寸]]を下賜された。[[5世紀]]に繁栄した[[葛城氏]]は[[武内宿禰]]の子孫で、姓も[[臣]]で別系統だが、婚姻関係によって同系統化している。
== 本拠 == === 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''葛城国'''と呼ばれた地域で、のちの大和国'''[[葛城]]'''地方（[[葛上郡]]・[[葛下郡]]両郡）にあたる。現在の[[奈良県]][[御所市]]・[[大和高田市]]。
== 氏神 == 不明。同地域には[[葛城御縣神社|葛城御県神社]]・[[葛城一言主神社|葛城坐一言主神社]]など延喜の制の名神大社・大社が連立。
== 人物 == *'''[[荒田彦]]'''（あらたひこ） *:古墳時代の人物で、娘の[[葛比売]]は皇別葛城氏の[[葛城襲津彦]]の母。
== 子孫 == *[[葛城磐村]] *:古墳時代の人。娘の広子は[[用明天皇]]の嬪となった。 *女（名称不明） *:[[蘇我稲目]]の妻（[[蘇我馬子]]の母）は葛城氏の出身と考えられている。 *[[葛城山田瑞子]] *:[[欽明天皇|欽明朝]]の人。稲目と共に[[児島郡]]へ田令として派遣された。 *葛木直安倍 *:年代は不明だが、平城宮跡出土木簡に「越前国江沼郡々里葛木直安倍」と名前が見える。
== 参考文献 == * 『[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|國史大辭典]]』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) * 『[[神道大辞典]]』([[臨川書店]]) * [[舎人親王]]『[[日本書紀]]』
== 関連項目 == *[[国造#一覧|国造の一覧]] *[[倭の六県]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:かすらきのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:大和国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:葛城氏|*]]

== 参考文献 == * 『[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|國史大辭典]]』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) * 『[[神道大辞典]]』([[臨川書店]]) * [[舎人親王]]『[[日本書紀]]』

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- Image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' * A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} * A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter * Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} * A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe * Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter * Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} * A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe * Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Katsuragi Kuni]] to [[Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- Image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' * A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} * A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter * Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} * A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe * Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}

His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- Image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- Image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- Image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- Image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- Image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}

== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- Image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- Image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- Image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: MAINSPACE to NAMESPACE
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- Image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
