revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.

== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}

'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.

In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.

However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.

His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' == See Also! == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}

'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.

The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.

This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.

His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.

In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.

This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目|12=draft}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|12=draft}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|12=draft}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王|12=draft}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県|12=draft}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)|12=draft}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命|12=draft}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別|12=draft}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神|12=draft}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国|12=draft}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国|12=draft}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直|12=draft}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=draft}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命|12=draft}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|12=draft}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|12=draft}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|12=draft}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|12=draft}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|12=draft}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|12=draft}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|12=draft}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|12=draft}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|12=draft}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目|12=draft}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|12=draft}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|12=draft}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王|12=draft}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県|12=draft}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|12=draft}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|12=draft}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|12=draft}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|12=draft}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|12=draft}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|12=draft}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|12=draft}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|12=draft}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|12=draft}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目|12=draft}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|12=draft}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|12=draft}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王|12=draft}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県|12=draft}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = <!-- image of the family crest --> | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)|12=draft}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命|12=draft}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別|12=draft}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天神|12=draft}}) | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国|12=draft}} | base = Kazuraki (later {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国|12=draft}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}} "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直|12=draft}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft}}. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=draft}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命|12=draft}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|12=draft}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|12=draft}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|12=draft}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|12=draft}}). This includes parts of present-day {{ill|Gose City|ja|御所市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|12=draft}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|12=draft}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|12=draft}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|12=draft}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|12=draft}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}}. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目|12=draft}} and mother of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|12=draft}} ** A figure from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}}, who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|12=draft}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * {{ill|Tōneri Shinnō|ja|舎人親王|12=draft}}, ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県|12=draft}}

Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|12=draft}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|12=draft}}, both lines merged through marriage alliances.

revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 |家名 = 葛城国造 |家紋 = <!-- 家紋の画像 --> |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[葛城氏 (葛城国造)|葛城氏]] |家祖 = [[剣根命]] |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天神]]） |出身地 = [[大和国|大倭国]] |根拠地 = 葛城（のちの[[大和国]]） |人物 = [[#人物]]参照 |支流 = [[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂氏]] }}
'''葛城国造'''（かずらきのくにのみやつこ・かずらきこくぞう・かつらぎのくにのみやつこ・かつらぎこくぞう）は[[葛城国]]（[[令制国]]の[[大和国]]南西部）を支配した[[国造]]。葛木国造とも言う。
== 概要 == 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」では'''葛城国造'''とある。
=== 祖先 === 『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」によれば葛城直の祖である'''[[剣根命]]'''が[[神武天皇]]朝に初代葛城国造に任命されたとある。『新撰姓氏録』によれば剣根命は'''[[高皇産霊尊]]'''の5世孫、[[天押立命]]の4世孫とされる。
=== 氏族 === '''[[葛城氏 (葛城国造)|葛城]]'''（かつらぎうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）。[[天武天皇]]12年([[683年]])に[[連]]を、14年に[[忌寸]]を下賜された。[[5世紀]]に繁栄した[[葛城氏]]は[[武内宿禰]]の子孫で、姓も[[臣]]で別系統だが、婚姻関係によって同系統化している。
== 本拠 == === 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''葛城国'''と呼ばれた地域で、のちの大和国'''[[葛城]]'''地方（[[葛上郡]]・[[葛下郡]]両郡）にあたる。現在の[[奈良県]][[御所市]]・[[大和高田市]]。
== 氏神 == 不明。同地域には[[葛城御縣神社|葛城御県神社]]・[[葛城一言主神社|葛城坐一言主神社]]など延喜の制の名神大社・大社が連立。
== 人物 == *'''[[荒田彦]]'''（あらたひこ） *:古墳時代の人物で、娘の[[葛比売]]は皇別葛城氏の[[葛城襲津彦]]の母。
== 子孫 == *[[葛城磐村]] *:古墳時代の人。娘の広子は[[用明天皇]]の嬪となった。 *女（名称不明） *:[[蘇我稲目]]の妻（[[蘇我馬子]]の母）は葛城氏の出身と考えられている。 *[[葛城山田瑞子]] *:[[欽明天皇|欽明朝]]の人。稲目と共に[[児島郡]]へ田令として派遣された。 *葛木直安倍 *:年代は不明だが、平城宮跡出土木簡に「越前国江沼郡々里葛木直安倍」と名前が見える。
== 参考文献 == * 『[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|國史大辭典]]』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) * 『[[神道大辞典]]』([[臨川書店]]) * [[舎人親王]]『[[日本書紀]]』
== 関連項目 == *[[国造#一覧|国造の一覧]] *[[倭の六県]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:かすらきのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:大和国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:葛城氏|*]]

=== 祖先 === 『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」によれば葛城直の祖である'''[[剣根命]]'''が[[神武天皇]]朝に初代葛城国造に任命されたとある。『新撰姓氏録』によれば剣根命は'''[[高皇産霊尊]]'''の5世孫、[[天押立命]]の4世孫とされる。
=== 氏族 === '''[[葛城氏 (葛城国造)|葛城]]'''（かつらぎうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）。[[天武天皇]]12年([[683年]])に[[連]]を、14年に[[忌寸]]を下賜された。[[5世紀]]に繁栄した[[葛城氏]]は[[武内宿禰]]の子孫で、姓も[[臣]]で別系統だが、婚姻関係によって同系統化している。
== 本拠 == === 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''葛城国'''と呼ばれた地域で、のちの大和国'''[[葛城]]'''地方（[[葛上郡]]・[[葛下郡]]両郡）にあたる。現在の[[奈良県]][[御所市]]・[[大和高田市]]。
== 氏神 == 不明。同地域には[[葛城御縣神社|葛城御県神社]]・[[葛城一言主神社|葛城坐一言主神社]]など延喜の制の名神大社・大社が連立。
== 人物 == *'''[[荒田彦]]'''（あらたひこ） *:古墳時代の人物で、娘の[[葛比売]]は皇別葛城氏の[[葛城襲津彦]]の母。
== 子孫 == *[[葛城磐村]] *:古墳時代の人。娘の広子は[[用明天皇]]の嬪となった。 *女（名称不明） *:[[蘇我稲目]]の妻（[[蘇我馬子]]の母）は葛城氏の出身と考えられている。 *[[葛城山田瑞子]] *:[[欽明天皇|欽明朝]]の人。稲目と共に[[児島郡]]へ田令として派遣された。 *葛木直安倍 *:年代は不明だが、平城宮跡出土木簡に「越前国江沼郡々里葛木直安倍」と名前が見える。
== 参考文献 == * 『[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|國史大辭典]]』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) * 『[[神道大辞典]]』([[臨川書店]]) * [[舎人親王]]『[[日本書紀]]』
== 関連項目 == *[[国造#一覧|国造の一覧]] *[[倭の六県]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:かすらきのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:大和国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:葛城氏|*]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi no Nekoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami lineage|qq=}} ([[:en:Tenjin|Amatsukami]]) | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Kazuraki (later [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|qq=}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城国造}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Omi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]], both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|qq=}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushimo District|qq=}}). This includes parts of present-day [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose City]] and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|qq=}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|qq=}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Daijiten|qq=}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Tōneri Shinnō]], ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Provinces of Wa|qq=}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Omi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]], both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|qq=}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushimo District|qq=}}). This includes parts of present-day [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose City]] and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|qq=}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|qq=}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Daijiten|qq=}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Tōneri Shinnō]], ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Provinces of Wa|qq=}}

In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Omi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]], both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|qq=}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushimo District|qq=}}). However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].

== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57538530}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Daijiten|qq=|1=Shintō Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590924}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Tōneri Shinnō]], ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Provinces of Wa|qq=|1=Six Agata in Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521849}}
