{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11620893]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11620893]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi no Nekoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami lineage|qq=|1=Shinbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588768}} ([[:en:Tenjin|Amatsukami]]) | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Kazuraki (later [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|qq=|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城国造|1=Kazuraki Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620893}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Omi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]], both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|qq=|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushimo District|qq=|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}). This includes parts of present-day [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose City]] and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620922}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57538530}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Daijiten|qq=|1=Shintō Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590924}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Tōneri Shinnō]], ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Provinces of Wa|qq=|1=Six Agata in Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521849}}

'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城国造|1=Kazuraki Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620893}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Omi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]], both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|qq=|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushimo District|qq=|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}). This includes parts of present-day [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose City]] and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620922}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57538530}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Daijiten|qq=|1=Shintō Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590924}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Tōneri Shinnō]], ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]''

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11620893]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11620893]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi no Nekoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami lineage|qq=|1=Shinbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588768}} ([[:en:Tenjin|Amatsukami]]) | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Kazuraki (later [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|qq=|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城国造|1=Kazuraki Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620893}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Omi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]], both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|qq=|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushimo District|qq=|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}). This includes parts of present-day [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose City]] and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620922}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57538530}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Daijiten|qq=|1=Shintō Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590924}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Tōneri Shinnō]], ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Provinces of Wa|qq=|1=Six Agata in Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521849}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城国造|1=Kazuraki Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620893}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Omi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]], both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|qq=|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushimo District|qq=|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}). This includes parts of present-day [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose City]] and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620922}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57538530}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Daijiten|qq=|1=Shintō Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590924}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Tōneri Shinnō]], ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Provinces of Wa|qq=|1=Six Agata in Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521849}}

'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城国造|1=Kazuraki Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620893}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Omi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]], both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|qq=|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushimo District|qq=|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}). This includes parts of present-day [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose City]] and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620922}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57538530}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.

This includes parts of present-day [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose City]] and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620922}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57538530}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Daijiten|qq=|1=Shintō Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590924}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Tōneri Shinnō]], ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Provinces of Wa|qq=|1=Six Agata in Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521849}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11620893]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11620893]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi no Nekoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami lineage|qq=|1=Shinbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588768}} ([[:en:Tenjin|Amatsukami]]) | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Kazuraki (later [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|qq=|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城国造|1=Kazuraki Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620893}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Omi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]], both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|qq=|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushimo District|qq=|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}). This includes parts of present-day [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose City]] and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620922}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57538530}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Daijiten|qq=|1=Shintō Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590924}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Tōneri Shinnō]], ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Provinces of Wa|qq=|1=Six Agata in Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521849}}

Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Omi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]], both lines merged through marriage alliances.

This includes parts of present-day [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose City]] and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture.

However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].

His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620922}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57538530}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Daijiten|qq=|1=Shintō Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590924}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Tōneri Shinnō]], ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]''

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:葛城国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11620893]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11620893]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi no Nekoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami lineage|qq=|1=Shinbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588768}} ([[:en:Tenjin|Amatsukami]]) | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Kazuraki (later [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|qq=|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城国造|1=Kazuraki Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620893}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki no Atae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Omi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]], both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|qq=|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushimo District|qq=|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}). This includes parts of present-day [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose City]] and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620922}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|qq=|1=Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57538530}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Daijiten|qq=|1=Shintō Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590924}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Tōneri Shinnō]], ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Provinces of Wa|qq=|1=Six Agata in Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521849}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:葛城国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620893]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620893]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko | mon = | mon_name = | honsei = {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja|葛城氏 (葛城国造)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | founder = {{ill|Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja|剣根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi no Nekoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Kami lineage|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami lineage|1=Shinbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588768}} ([[:en:Tenjin|Amatsukami]]) | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Kazuraki (later [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]) | notable_figures = See [[#Notable figures]] | branches = {{ill|Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan (Kamo no Asomi)|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} }}
'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城国造|1=Kazuraki Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620893}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki no Atae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]], both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushimo District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}). This includes parts of present-day [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose City]] and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratahiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620922}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|1=Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57538530}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Daijiten|1=Shintō Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590924}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Tōneri Shinnō]], ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Provinces of Wa|1=Six Agata in Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521849}}

'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城国造|1=Kazuraki Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620893}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki no Atae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]], both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushimo District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}). This includes parts of present-day [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose City]] and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratahiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620922}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|1=Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57538530}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Daijiten|1=Shintō Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590924}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Tōneri Shinnō]], ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]''

The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]], both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushimo District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}). This includes parts of present-day [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose City]] and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratahiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620922}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|1=Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57538530}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Daijiten|1=Shintō Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590924}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Tōneri Shinnō]], ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]''
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Six Provinces of Wa|ja|倭の六県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Provinces of Wa|1=Six Agata in Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521849}}

'''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (葛城国造, also read as Kazuraki Kokuzō or Katsuragi Kokuzō) was the ruler of {{ill|Kazuraki Province|ja|葛城国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城国造|1=Kazuraki Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620893}} (the southwestern part of the Yamato Province under the [[Ritsuryō]] system). They are also referred to as '''Kazuraki Kokuzō'''.
== Overview == The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kokuzō Hongi" refers to them as '''Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Tsurugi no Nekoto, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kazuraki no Atae|ja|葛城直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki no Atae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed the first Kazuraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' states that Tsurugi no Nekoto was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} and a fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja|天押立命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaoshitatemi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Clan === The Kazuraki clan (葛城氏, surname: ''Atae''). In {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}'s 12th year (683), the clan was granted the surname ''Muraji'' and later ''Imiki'' in the 14th year. Although the {{ill|Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja|臣姓葛城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki clan (Omi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that prospered in the 5th century descended from [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]], both lines merged through marriage alliances.
== Territory == === Governing Area === The Miyatsuko ruled over the area known at the time as ''Kazuraki Province,'' corresponding to the Kazuraki region in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}} and {{ill|Katsushimo District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushimo District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}). This includes parts of present-day [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose City]] and [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture.
== Clan Deity == The clan deity is unknown. However, the region contains several prominent shrines, including {{ill|Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja|葛城御縣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja|葛城坐一言主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Hitokotonushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both classified as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Aratahiko|ja|荒田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratahiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** A figure from the Kofun period. His daughter, {{ill|Kazuhime|ja|葛比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the mother of {{ill|Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|ja|葛城襲津彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620922}} of the imperial Kazuraki clan.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja|葛城磐村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Iwamura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** A Kofun-period figure. His daughter Hiroko became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. * Unnamed Daughter ** Believed to be the wife of [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] and mother of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]]. * {{ill|Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|ja|葛城山田瑞子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|1=Katsuragi Yamada Mizuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q57538530}} ** A figure from the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], who was dispatched alongside Iname to Kibi Province as an administrator. * Katsuragi no Atae Abe ** Referenced in wooden tablets unearthed at the Heijō Palace site as ''Kosho District Katsuragi no Atae Abe'' of Etchū Province.
== References == * ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}) * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|author=Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} * ''Nihon Kodaijinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''{{ill|Shintō Daijiten|ja|神道大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintō Daijiten|1=Shintō Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590924}}'' (Rinsen Shoten) * [[:en:Prince Toneri|Tōneri Shinnō]], ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]''
